title: Best-path gives no 'EB-1A is the higher bar' reasoning and frames the green card as a bonus — can confidently steer a self-petitioner toward the wrong path
expected: For an EB-1A self-petitioner, the best-path comparison should reason O-1A vs EB-1A for his facts and be candid that EB-1A is the harder green-card bar (sustained acclaim / final-merits 'small percentage at the top'), not a coin-flip — a senior intake memo talks the optimist down.
got: rationaleFor() ranks all three live programs by qualifying margin against an IDENTICAL threshold of 3 (packs.ts:97,142), with an identical likelihood formula regardless of classification (qualification.ts:241-242). The only EB-1A-specific signal is appending 'It is also a green card (permanent residence)' (best-path.ts:120-121), which frames EB-1A as an UPSIDE, the opposite of the higher-bar warning. No code encodes that EB-1A is harder than O-1A anywhere in the path.
suggested_acceptance: Best-path differentiates EB-1A's higher bar from O-1A in its rationale (e.g. surfaces the final-merits standard / a higher effective threshold or a calibrated likelihood) and does not present the green card purely as a sweetener; on a borderline founder record it does not recommend EB-1A over O-1A without flagging the elevated bar.
l2_priority: Run Seun's real founder record through best-path live and assert the recommendation rationale reasons O-1A-vs-EB-1A and acknowledges EB-1A's elevated final-merits bar rather than dangling 'green card'.

title: Best-path comparison runs the deterministic keyword mock, not the model, even in the live environment
expected: A founder weighing a multi-thousand-dollar visa-strategy decision expects the comparison he relies on to use the real model's judgement of his whole record.
got: /api/qualify/preview/best-path runs recommendBestPath -> scoreAllPrograms -> mockQualification (keyword heuristic) only; no charge, no model, no persistence (best-path.ts:75-98; api/qualify/preview/best-path/route.ts:11-18). An off-keyword strong founder signal can be under-scored on the very comparison that drives his path choice. (Prior run captured the under-scoring angle as SR-QV-01; this records it from the EB-1A-decision angle.)
suggested_acceptance: Either back the best-path comparison with the model on the authenticated path, or make the keyword-preview-vs-model distinction explicit on the best-path UI so the founder knows the path recommendation is a quick read, not the model's full judgement.
l2_priority: Confirm whether the authenticated post-handoff screening meaningfully re-reads the record (model) and whether the keyword nature of the comparison is disclosed at the decision point.

title: DraftStudio idle copy hardcodes 'Draft a full O-1A petition letter' while correctly drafting EB-1A
expected: An EB-1A petitioner should see EB-1A (or visa-agnostic) copy in the drafting studio; seeing 'O-1A' for a man primed to catch an O-1A fallback is a trust double-take.
got: The studio's idle/error blurb reads 'Draft a full O-1A petition letter from your scored criteria...' regardless of classification, even though the actual prompt header correctly uses req.classification ('U.S. EB-1A immigration petition letter', drafting.ts:177) and the draft is genuinely EB-1A. Cosmetic copy, not the prompt — but it smells like the very fallback he distrusts.
suggested_acceptance: DraftStudio idle/help copy interpolates the case classification (or is visa-agnostic) so an EB-1A user never sees 'O-1A' while building an EB-1A petition.
l2_priority: Screenshot the DraftStudio idle state on an EB-1A case and confirm the copy no longer says O-1A.

title: Draft integrity stack: citation discipline + live fabrication gate + section-continuity context
expected: Zero fabricated revenue/round/Forbes facts; no inventing criteria he didn't claim; regenerate stays consistent with the rest of the letter.
got: Prompt forbids invented specifics and fences CASE_DATA against injection (drafting.ts:180-200); a live adjudication gate flags money/percent/year/big-int specifics present in output but absent from input (adjudication-gates.ts:83-90); only Met/Strong criteria earn a section, so 'Artistic exhibitions' (None for a fintech founder) gets no hallucinated section; per-section regenerate now passes the other sections as read-only LETTER_CONTEXT (G1.1/dc-draft-02 landed, drafting.ts:222-267) and pickMergeBase preserves unsaved edits.
suggested_acceptance: Keep the fabrication gate, citation discipline, and section-continuity context; do not weaken the DISCLAIMER or the Met/Strong-only section gating.
l2_priority: Verify the fabrication gate fires on an injected fake ARR/Forbes-year and the live EB-1A draft names his real supplied specifics.

title: Landing reads as an O-1-only product; 'EB-1A' never appears on '/' though it is fully built
expected: A founder buying for the green card should see, on the cold landing read, that the tool serves EB-1A — the permanence he arrived for.
got: page.tsx is O-1-centric throughout: 'Your O-1 visa, drafted with care' (:133), 'the eight O-1 criteria' (:92,154,250,282), hero card stamped 'O-1A . Sciences' (:199,210). The string 'EB-1A' appears nowhere on '/'. EB-1A is only discoverable inside /qualify's best-path finder or the FAQ. It does not block the job (EB-1A is reachable and fully built) but undersells his exact use case and makes him work to confirm the product is for him.
suggested_acceptance: The landing names EB-1A (the green-card self-petition) alongside O-1, so a permanence-seeking founder sees on the cold read that the tool serves his case.
l2_priority: Confirm the landing communicates EB-1A support and that a prospect can find the green-card path without digging into /qualify.

title: Positioning + UPL line + primary-source validation are consistent and credible
expected: Unmistakable 'drafting tool, not a law firm; your attorney signs; not legal advice', transparent self-serve pricing, and validation that shows evidence not adjectives.
got: Consistent positioning across landing/FAQ/billing (page.tsx:155-156,255,372-373; faq:27,31,43); single canonical DISCLAIMER on every AI payload (result.ts:37-41); /pricing redirects to /billing with bundles sourced from economy.ts BUNDLES so prices can't drift (pricing/page.tsx:9; page.tsx:13,328,442); /validation cites primary law per program, EB-1A verified to 8 CFR 204.5(h)(3) with last-reviewed dates + CI freshness gate (validation.ts:103-126).
suggested_acceptance: Protect the consistent UPL/positioning line, canonical DISCLAIMER, economy-sourced pricing, and the primary-source validation page.
l2_priority: Spot-check the rendered /validation and /billing for an EB-1A prospect; confirm no price drift and the EB-1A legal basis renders.

title: Share token leaks nothing private; pack-length validated; honest informational framing
expected: A board/counsel-shareable verdict that reads credibly and leaks nothing he didn't choose, framed as informational not a legal grant.
got: The token encodes only name/classification/likelihood/per-criterion statuses in pack order, never the profile text (letters-patent.ts:69-77). decodeSnapshot validates the status count matches the pack length (an EB-1A token must carry exactly 10 or it 404s, :97) and only live programs decode (:93). /c/[token] renders from the token with no DB, shows the 10 EB-1A criteria as a coat-of-arms, footers 'Informational only . not legal advice . no account needed' (c/[token]/page.tsx:103-107,119); OG card falls back gracefully. The 'Certificate of Extraordinary Ability' framing is the known PN-QUAL-01/G3.1 polish item, referenced not re-filed.
suggested_acceptance: Keep the snapshot-only token (no profile text), the pack-length validation, and the informational/not-legal-advice framing.
l2_priority: Confirm the rendered EB-1A certificate shows 10 criteria and the OG card unfurls without exposing private data.

title: Roadmap and real-case list derive from actual case state; user is never stranded
expected: Dashboard lists his real EB-1A case (above the mock demo); case detail shows accurate stage/roadmap; deep links hydrate; always an obvious next action.
got: YourCasesCard renders the user's real DB cases above the mock demo with classification/status/likelihood (CaseFileDashboard.tsx:31,136-172; dashboard/page.tsx:30-38); empty state points to /qualify (:112-130). caseRoadmap derives done/current/upcoming purely from real status + hasEvidence/hasDraft (roadmap.ts:38-58; CaseDetailView.tsx:133-137). 'Open case file' deep link hydrates the detail route from saved state. Cosmetic-only: the dashboard top bar context is hardcoded mock chrome 'O1-241 . Krishnan . O-1A' (DashboardView.tsx:32).
suggested_acceptance: Protect the real-case-list-above-mock pattern and the state-derived roadmap; optionally drop the hardcoded mock label from the top bar so a real case isn't briefly misread.
l2_priority: Walk a real EB-1A case through Drafting -> Review -> Filed and confirm the roadmap stage tracks each action.
